prompt: Trace how Flask receives a WSGI request, opens request handling, dispatches to a view, finalizes the response, and returns control to the server.

expected_symbols.name: Flask.wsgi_app
expected_symbols.why: WSGI entry point for request handling.

expected_symbols.name: Flask.full_dispatch_request
expected_symbols.why: Coordinates preprocessing, dispatch, error handling, and finalization.

expected_symbols.name: Flask.dispatch_request
expected_symbols.why: Finds and invokes the matched view function.

expected_symbols.name: Scaffold.route
expected_symbols.why: Decorator path used to register view functions.

expected_claims.text: Flask.wsgi_app is the WSGI entry point and creates or uses request context before dispatch.
full_dispatch_request wraps preprocessing, dispatch, exception handling, and response finalization.
dispatch_request invokes the view function selected by URL matching.
The route decorator registers view functions through the scaffold URL rule path rather than performing request dispatch itself.

forbidden_claims.text: The route decorator directly handles each incoming WSGI request.
Flask dispatch does not use request context.
